name: HR_API_HOOK_CALLS
description: Lists which extra logic, package procedures or formula, should be called from the API hook points. This table will contain rows created by legislation groups, legislation vertical market groups and customers.

columns.Name: API_HOOK_CALL_ID
columns.Comments: System generated primary key column.

columns.Name: API_HOOK_ID
columns.Comments: Foreign key to HR_API_HOOKS.

columns.Name: API_HOOK_CALL_TYPE
columns.Comments: Type of hook call.

columns.Name: LEGISLATION_CODE
columns.Comments: Indicates if the extra logic should be called every time the hook point is reached or when the data corresponds to a particular legislation.

columns.Name: SEQUENCE
columns.Comments: When more than one row exists for the same API_HOOK_ID, SEQUENCE affects the order of the hook calls.

columns.Name: ENABLED_FLAG
columns.Comments: Indicates if the hook call should be generated.

columns.Name: CALL_PACKAGE
columns.Comments: Name of the database package which the hook package should call to carry out the legislation, legislation vertical market or customer specific logic

columns.Name: CALL_PROCEDURE
columns.Comments: Name of the procedure, within CALL_PACKAGE, which the hook package should call to carry out the legislation, legislation vertical market or customer specific logic.

columns.Name: PRE_PROCESSOR_DATE
columns.Comments: The last time the API hook pre-processor program attempted (successfully or unsuccessfully) to create the package body code for this hook call.

columns.Name: ENCODED_ERROR
columns.Comments: If the last time the API hook pre-processor program failed to make the source code for this hook call, the error details will be held in this column.

columns.Name: STATUS
columns.Comments: Status of hook call code at pre-processor time (as of PRE_PROCESSOR_DATE).

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: APPLICATION_ID
columns.Comments: The APPLICATION_ID of Application as defined in the FND_APPLICATION table.

columns.Name: APP_INSTALL_STATUS
columns.Comments: Indicates for which installation statuses the hook call should be included in the user hook package body.

columns.Name: SEED_DATA_SOURCE
columns.Comments: Source of seed data record. A value of 'BULK_SEED_DATA_SCRIPT' indicates that record was bulk loaded. Otherwise, specifies the name of the seed data file.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET1
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET1. Context values are Y or N.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET2
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET2. Context values are Y or N.

indexes.Columns: APPLICATION_ID, API_HOOK_ID, LEGISLATION_CODE, CALL_PACKAGE, CALL_PROCEDURE, ORA_SEED_SET1
APPLICATION_ID, API_HOOK_ID, LEGISLATION_CODE, CALL_PACKAGE, CALL_PROCEDURE, ORA_SEED_SET2
